sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a right knee injury following a plank kick during a pickup soccer game three days ago. He describes the pain as 6-7 on a pain scale and reports using ibuprofen regularly. He states the knee feels loose and limited in range of motion, particularly with bending and straightening. He reports difficulty bearing weight and a noticeable limp. He reports some swelling that has decreased but still feels pain with palpation at the medial joint line.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient exhibits a marked limp upon ambulation. Physical exam reveals significant effusion in the right knee and ecchymosis. Passive range of motion is limited, with terminal extension at 115 degrees and terminal flexion at 20-30 degrees secondary to pain. Lachman's test is positive. Neurovascular exam is intact. Muscle strength is 5/5 bilaterally.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's presentation is highly suggestive of an anterior cruciate ligament tear.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order an MRI of the right knee to evaluate the integrity of the ACL. Prescribe meloxicam 50 mg daily for pain and inflammation. Instruct the patient to ice the knee as needed. Order a knee immobilizer and crutches for weight-bearing. Recommend moderate activity modification, limiting work activities and slowing jogging. Schedule follow-up to discuss MRI results and determine the need for surgical intervention.
